title: Godot Bootstrap Medium — Arena Sweep Physics2D End-to-End
summary: A medium-difficulty bootstrap challenge combining Godot engine repair and a deterministic headless mini-game. The engine side requires restoring hollowed Physics2D modules (SAT solver, shape geometry, collision dispatcher, space queries, body dynamics, body pair solver, area pair solver, physics step), while the game side (GDScript) must be rebuilt to drive combat through overlap queries and raycast-based projectiles, producing deterministic, verifiable replay output.
instructionPreview: You are given: - A Godot source tree at /workspace/godot - A deterministic headless mini-game project at /workspace/game_project (Arena Sweep) - Replay and expected-output assets under /workspace/assets
instruction:
You are given:
- A Godot source tree at /workspace/godot
- A deterministic headless mini-game project at /workspace/game_project (Arena Sweep)
- Replay and expected-output assets under /workspace/assets

Your task is to complete a full bootstrap workflow:

Phase A - Engine and game repair, then verification
1) Complete intentionally hollowed Godot Physics2D query-chain implementation.
   The task contains large missing implementations and hidden bugs on the same
   functional call chain. You must retrieve code context across modules and restore
   correct behavior end-to-end.
1.1) The engine side includes BOTH:
   - Large missing functionality (>= 2500 lines of engine-related implementation).
   - Additional compilable logic bugs on the same call chain that still require fixing.
1.2) Complete the game-side scripts under /workspace/game_project/scripts (replay runner,
   replay I/O, scan/overlap helper, simulation world). Together they are on the order
   of ~500 lines of GDScript. The game is a partial skeleton with an intentional seed
   mismatch bug in the replay runner; final behavior must be deterministic and
   physics-query-driven (intersect_shape / overlap queries), not animation-based.
2) Build a headless Godot binary with unit test support enabled.
3) Run a selected set of Godot engine tests and save a machine-readable report.

Phase B - Game replay verification
4) Run the headless mini-game replay twice using the same seed and replay input
   (the harness enables strict checks that physics overlap and deterministic RNG were used).
   The game is intentionally query-driven:
   - Combat scans use Physics2D overlap queries (intersect_shape).
   - Enemy behavior depends on deterministic RNG.
   - Replay output must include scan/hit/spawn/kill events (see expected event types under assets).
5) Export final state JSON and its SHA256 hash for each run.
6) Confirm replay determinism (two same-seed runs produce identical state hash).

Phase C - Integrated acceptance
7) Produce all required output artifacts listed below.
8) Ensure outputs match expected integrity checks.

Required output files:
- /workspace/output/engine_checks.json
- /workspace/output/engine_test_stdout.log
- /workspace/output/replay_run_1/final_state.json
- /workspace/output/replay_run_1/final_state.sha256
- /workspace/output/replay_run_1/combat_events.jsonl
- /workspace/output/replay_run_1/engine_trace.json
- /workspace/output/replay_run_2/final_state.json
- /workspace/output/replay_run_2/final_state.sha256
- /workspace/output/replay_run_2/combat_events.jsonl
- /workspace/output/replay_run_2/engine_trace.json
- /workspace/output/game_final_state.json
- /workspace/output/game_final_state.sha256
- /workspace/output/determinism_report.json

Rules:
- Automated grading (pytest) checks engine_checks.json, combat event types, engine_trace flags,
  replay determinism, and compares the run SHA256 to a reference value stored only under /tests
  (not in the agent workspace).
- Do not edit files under /tests.
- Do not bypass replay execution.
- Do not fabricate hashes; compute them from the actual JSON outputs you generate.
- The final hash must be computed from canonicalized JSON content.
- Do not replace query-driven combat logic with manual state mutation.
- Do not edit files under /workspace/game_project/tests (if present).
- Do not bypass engine-side missing implementations by stubbing outputs.
- Engine fixes must be made on the Physics2D query chain used by the game.

moduleDag.project: Godot Bootstrap Medium — Arena Sweep Physics2D End-to-End
moduleDag.description: A medium-difficulty bootstrap challenge combining Godot engine repair and a deterministic headless mini-game. The engine side requires restoring hollowed Physics2D modules (SAT solver, shape geometry, collision dispatcher, space queries, body dynamics, body pair solver, area pair solver, physics step), while the game side (GDScript) must be rebuilt to drive combat through overlap queries and raycast-based projectiles, producing deterministic, verifiable replay output.

nodes.label: SAT Collision Solver (2D)
nodes.description: The low-level Separating Axis Theorem (SAT) collision detection functions for all 2D shape pairs. Entirely hollowed; must be fully restored as the foundation of all shape overlap tests.

nodes.label: Shape Geometry Functions (2D)
nodes.description: Shape-specific geometry implementations: contains_point, intersect_segment, get_supports for Circle, Rectangle, Capsule, ConvexPolygon, and ConcavePolygon shapes. Also includes ConcavePolygon BVH generation and culling. Foundation for all collision detection.

nodes.label: Replay I/O Utility
nodes.description: Standalone GDScript utility that loads replay JSON files, normalises their schema, and writes canonicalised JSON/JSONL output. Has no dependencies on other patched units.

nodes.label: Collision Solver Dispatcher
nodes.description: The mid-level collision solver that inspects shape type pairs and dispatches to the correct SAT template instantiation. Also handles the callback-based collision-test interface used by higher-level space queries.

nodes.label: Body Dynamics (Force/Velocity Integration)
nodes.description: Implements integrate_forces (gravity, damping, force application, kinematic motion) and integrate_velocities (position/rotation update from velocity). Required by the physics step pipeline.

nodes.label: Physics2D Space Query Chain
nodes.description: Implements GodotPhysicsDirectSpaceState2D: intersect_point, intersect_ray, intersect_shape, rest_info, and motion_shape. The primary query interface called by the GDScript layer via the physics server.

nodes.label: Body Pair Contact Solver
nodes.description: Handles body-body contact resolution: contact callbacks, validation, continuous collision detection (CCD), constraint setup, pre-solve, and iterative solve.

nodes.label: Area Pair Overlap Solver
nodes.description: Detects and reports overlap between Area2D nodes and bodies/other areas. Manages monitor callbacks and space override attachments.

nodes.label: Physics Step Pipeline
nodes.description: Orchestrates the full physics step: force integration, broadphase update, island generation, constraint setup/pre-solve/solve, velocity integration, and sleep/wake management.

nodes.label: Physics Server Logic Bug Fixes
nodes.description: Fixes two compilable logic bugs: an off-by-one error in intersect_shape_on_canvas, and a swapped safe/unsafe return pair in cast_motion.

nodes.label: Scan System (Physics Overlap Wrapper)
nodes.description: GDScript class that constructs a CircleShape2D query, calls PhysicsDirectSpaceState2D.intersect_shape, and normalises hit results.

nodes.label: Projectile System (Raycast Combat)
nodes.description: GDScript class managing projectile lifecycle: fire with spread, advance positions, raycast hit detection via intersect_ray, lifetime expiration.

nodes.label: Simulation World (Arena Sweep)
nodes.description: The core Arena Sweep simulation: spawns enemies, moves player, fires scan and projectile combat, updates enemy AI with line-of-sight, tracks HP/events/kills, and exports final state and engine trace.

nodes.label: Replay Runner Entry Point
nodes.description: SceneTree entry point that parses CLI arguments, loads a replay via ReplayIO, constructs SimWorld, runs the simulation, verifies trace flags, and writes output. Contains a seed-mismatch bug (seed+1) that must be corrected.

edges.label: collision_solver dispatches to SAT template instantiations for each shape-pair type
collision solver uses shape geometry functions for all narrow-phase tests
space query methods call GodotCollisionSolver2D for narrow-phase shape tests
body pair setup calls collision solver to detect contact points
area pair setup calls collision solver to detect overlap
physics step iterates body pair constraints
physics step calls integrate_forces and integrate_velocities
physics step processes area pair constraints
server-layer bugs only surface once space queries return correct results
GDScript intersect_shape calls require correct server bindings
projectile raycast queries use intersect_ray through the server layer
SimWorld delegates overlap queries to ScanSystem
SimWorld delegates raycast logic to ProjectileSystem
replay_runner uses ReplayIO to load replays and write output
replay_runner instantiates SimWorld for simulation execution
